mainEntity.name: My boss in Colorado is mad I reported a falling debris injury. Can they fire me for this?

acceptedAnswer.text: It is illegal to fire an employee for filing a workers' comp claim in Colorado, Utah, or Idaho. If you face retaliation, you may have grounds for a wrongful termination lawsuit in addition to your injury claim.
acceptedAnswer.description:
In Utah, Idaho, and Colorado, it is illegal for an employer to fire or discipline you solely for filing a workers' compensation claim. This is known as "retaliatory discharge." If your boss is angry that you reported a falling debris injury and begins to cut your hours, move you to a worse shift, or fires you, you may have a secondary legal claim for wrongful termination.

However, employers often use "neutral" reasons like "downsizing" or "performance issues" to mask retaliation. This makes it crucial to keep a record of all interactions with your boss after the injury.

Key factors include the timing of the firing relative to your claim and any documentation of your past job performance. If you feel you are being targeted for reporting an accident, you need a lawyer immediately to protect your job and your workers' comp benefits. Protecting your rights isn't just about the money; it's about holding the employer accountable for a safe workplace.
